title: caesalpinia pinnata subsp. oblongifolia
intro: Caesalpinia pinnata subsp. oblongifolia, also known as the 'prickly bush', is a type of flowering plant native to tropical regions, particularly in Southeast Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which have been found to aid in wound healing, reducing fever, and alleviating various health issues. The health benefits of Caesalpinia pinnata subsp. oblongifolia include its potential to lower blood sugar levels, reduce pain and inflammation, and improve immune function. Traditionally, it is prepared as a tea or infusion, with some practitioners also using it as a tincture or applying its leaves directly to affected areas.
